items.title: Agent at correspondents' dinner was hit by bullet from suspect's shotgun, prosecutors say
items.summary_auto: Authorities have determined that buckshot from the gun of the man charged with trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in an attempt to kill U.S. President Donald Trump struck a Secret Service agent, according to the federal prosecutor overseeing the investigation.

items.title: Cargo ship near Strait of Hormuz reports being attacked, as Iran makes new peace proposal
items.summary_auto: A cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz has reported being attacked by multiple small craft, the British military's United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations centre said Sunday, marking at least two dozen attacks in and around the strait since the Iran war began.

items.title: Poilievre argues Carney has 'wasted an entire year' on possible Alberta pipeline
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre says the prime minister has "wasted an entire year" deliberating a potential new oil pipeline out of Alberta — criticism that comes after Mark Carney said a new pipeline is "more probable than possible."

items.title: Raptors, Cavaliers get ready to face off in Game 7 showdown
items.summary_auto: Raptors get ready to face the Cavaliers for the final time this season in Game 7 of the first round of NBA playoffs.

items.title: Wildfire destroys homes in Sandy Beach Summer Village, Alta., prompting local state of emergency
items.summary_auto: According to a social media update from Sturgeon County posted Saturday, the fire has "consumed three homes and badly damaged another." Firefighting crews from multiple surrounding jurisdictions have been deployed to the scene.

items.title: Shakira performs free concert to a crowd of 2 million on Brazil's Copacabana Beach
items.summary_auto: Colombian superstar Shakira gave a free concert on Copacabana Beach in Rio de Janeiro on Saturday night, an event that the city's mayor said drew two million people to one of the world's most iconic waterfronts.

items.title: Press freedom is at a 25-year low, says Reporters Without Borders
items.summary_auto: Worldwide press freedom has fallen to its lowest level in 25 years, according to NGO Reporters Without Borders. For the first time since its World Press Freedom Index launched in 2002, more than half of the world’s countries fall into the 'difficult' or 'very serious' categories for press freedom.

items.title: Ukraine hits Russian oil-loading port, 'shadow fleet' tankers
items.summary_auto: Ukraine on Sunday launched a wave of strikes against Russian oil targets, hitting a key loading port on the Baltic Sea and two tankers that Ukraine alleges were illegally used to transport Russian crude.

items.title: Alberta government says wild horse population has reached 'unacceptable' levels, advocates disagree
items.summary_auto: The abundance of what Alberta's government calls "feral horses" has reached "unacceptable" population levels, according to the province's horse management strategy. Results from the province's annual feral horse survey released in April found the highest free-roaming horse numbers ever counted along the eastern slopes of the Rocky Mountains.

items.title: 'Build from the ashes’: Fort McMurray reflects on how community has changed since 2016 wildfire
items.summary_auto: It's been 10 years since more than 2,500 homes were destroyed in the 2016 Horse River Wildfire. The community has rebuilt and fire officials have invested millions of dollars into fire prevention and planning. Many residents are ready to move on, while others still feel they are in survival mode.

items.title: This N.L. pharmacist lost a ring 20 years ago. An Ontario man found it in a pair of jeans
items.summary_auto: After finding a golden ring in a thrifted pair of jeans in Ontario, Dan Howard spent 15 years tracking down its bearer. The owner, pharmacist Bradley Buckle, turned out to be half the country away in St. John's.

items.title: Workplace health and safety funding stagnant in N.L. as calls for stronger enforcement continue
items.summary_auto: The Newfoundland and Labrador government has not added funds to its occupational health and safety division, in spite of calls from the provincial federation of labour to do so.

items.title: NASA says it will put humans on the surface of the moon in 2028. How realistic is that?
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II lunar mission is over, and NASA is already looking ahead, planning to put astronauts on the surface of the moon by 2028. But there are a lot of hurdles that need to be cleared before that happens.

items.title: In Pompeii, ancient graffiti depicting daily life is being revealed thanks to modern technology
items.summary_auto: The walls of a corridor that once linked two theatres in the ancient Roman city of Pompeii hold hundreds of inscriptions made by residents before Mount Vesuvius erupted almost 2,000 years ago. An international research team is now using a very modern technique to bring them to life.

items.title: I didn't want to become my alcoholic father. I know, for better or worse, I never will be
items.summary_auto: Fernanda Rodriguez got a shocking call from her father when she was just nine — a call that opened her eyes to the many challenges her father faced when it came to alcoholism and depression. As she grew older, she was able to see that he persevered through difficulties to be present for her as much as he could be.

items.title: Jeff Bezos to Beyoncé: What to know ahead of the 2026 Met Gala
items.summary_auto: One of New York's hottest events is under pressure this year — not from declining attendance, glamour or budget — but from criticism, bad press and even calls for a boycott.

items.title: Jerome Blake injured, Canada places 7th in men's 100m final at World Athletics Relays
items.summary_auto: Jerome Blake of Kelowna, B.C., favoured his right leg while pulling up in the second leg of the men's 4x100-metre final, then hobbled to hand the baton to Brendon Rodney as Canada placed seventh in the eight-team field in 40.06 seconds on Sunday at the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone, Botswana.

items.title: Federal co-working sites may be ‘reallocated’ to meet 4-day office return
items.summary_auto: The federal government may tap into its shared workstations so that departments that are short on space can meet the goal of having unionized employees report to the office four days a week starting July 6.

items.title: Canada's new sovereign wealth fund: What Ottawa can learn from Alberta
items.summary_auto: Alberta launched its sovereign wealth fund decades ago, but it was regularly poached by politicians, which is why the bank balance failed to meaningfully grow.

items.title: Former teen track prodigy Mary Cain is exposing a culture of abuse that nearly destroyed her
items.summary_auto: Former teen track prodigy, Mary Cain, delves into the years of psychological abuse and body shaming that forced her to leave the sport she loved.

items.title: Amid growing calls to limit teens' use of AI chatbots, are parental controls enough?
items.summary_auto: Concerns are growing about how some young people engage with AI chatbots. Meta is rolling out a tool to let parents monitor topics their children discuss at the same time as Manitoba is proposing banning use of AI chatbots for youth. More research is needed, but evidence suggests some risk factors for mental health.

items.title: Youth knee surgeries are rising in Quebec. Here's how to prevent them
items.summary_auto: The number of patients under 18 years old who have undergone anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) repair surgery is on the rise in the province. A Montreal orthopedic surgeon says it’s because sports are getting more competitive.

items.title: Mother of boy who died in care of Ontario couple speaks for 1st time ahead of trial decision
items.summary_auto: In 2021, a 12-year-old died while in the care of a Burlington, Ont., couple who are now charged with his murder. For the first time, the boy's mother is sharing her perspective with CBC Hamilton, saying she wants people to understand that her family is "not just a case or story."

items.title: As Carney visits Armenia, experts urge deeper Canada ties in the Caucasus
items.summary_auto: Mark Carney is expected to spend the bulk of his few hours in Armenia engaging with European leaders, but experts say he has momentum to build ties with Yerevan and the rest of the Caucasus as well, something first undertaken by Trudeau.

items.title: Summer McIntosh sizzles at Florida meet with pair of freestyle victories
items.summary_auto: For as dominant as Canadian swimming superstar Summer McIntosh has been for the last number of years, there’s no sign of complacency or let up from the 19-year-old from Toronto.
